meaning: adj.炫耀的,过分装饰的,(色彩、装饰、打扮等)俗气的
examples: They climbed the garish, purple-carpeted stairs.
This colour is bright but not garish.
The girl was short and thin and her clothes were garish.
The garish filigree of the gonzo director, Terry Gilliam, surrounds this fable of redemption with pseudo-originality.
Decades before garish LED screens presided over the Strip, handcrafted neon signs reigned as Vegas' original iconic art form.
